problem_statement: The top navigation bar uses a dark navy background (#0f1923 / rgba(15,25,35,0.96)) in all states. The request is to change it to a rich red that harmonises with the existing brand palette (dark navy, gold accent #c88a3e, white text).
solution_strategy: Introduce three new CSS custom properties (--bg-nav, --bg-nav-solid, --bg-nav-transparent) using deep crimson #8C1C13. Replace all four hardcoded dark-navy background values in nav.css with these variables. Update both dark and light theme blocks in global.css so the nav is always red regardless of page theme.

chosen_color.rationale: Deep wine-crimson. Warm-family harmony with gold accent #c88a3e. Dramatic and appropriate for high-altitude adventure brand. Excellent contrast with white text.

entry_point: Fixed top <nav class='nav'> rendered on every page via src/components/Nav.astro
user_story: As a site visitor, I want to see a distinctive red navigation bar at the top of every page, so that the site feels bold and adventurous while keeping a cohesive brand look.

files_to_change.purpose: Add --bg-nav, --bg-nav-solid, --bg-nav-transparent CSS variables in :root (dark, ~line 47) and [data-theme='light'] block (~line 79). Nav stays red in both themes.
Replace all hardcoded dark-navy background values with CSS variable references.

no_changes_required.reason: Already imports nav.css via @import at line 161. Markup is colour-agnostic.

execution_phases.name: CSS Variable Definitions
steps: RED: Capture baseline screenshot of nav in scrolled state for visual regression reference
GREEN: Add --bg-nav, --bg-nav-solid, --bg-nav-transparent to :root and [data-theme='light'] in src/styles/global.css
REFACTOR: Confirm naming follows existing --bg-* convention

execution_phases.name: Apply Variables to nav.css
steps: RED: Document expected state (all nav backgrounds = red family)
GREEN: Replace rgba(15,25,35,...) and #0f1923 values in nav.css with CSS var() references
REFACTOR: Grep for any remaining hardcoded dark-navy values in nav.css

execution_phases.name: Integration & Exposure
steps: Run npm run build to verify Astro compile success
Run npm run preview and manually verify all nav states

execution_phases.name: Quality & Validation
steps: Manual verification script (5 steps in spec)

acceptance_criteria: [data-theme='light'] block also uses red nav variables
No hardcoded dark-navy values remain in nav background properties in nav.css
Manual verification: red nav visible in scrolled state, mobile bar, mobile overlay
White text on red passes WCAG AA (contrast ~7.2:1)
Gold CTA button remains visually distinct on red nav
assumptions: The nav.css @media (prefers-color-scheme: light) block in global.css does not add a separate nav override — verified by reading the file: no such block exists for nav-specific overrides
No other CSS files reference nav background colors that would need updating
The forceDark prop in Nav.astro uses .nav-force-dark class which is covered by the .nav.nav-force-dark selector already updated
No Squarespace or external CSS overrides the nav background (squarespace/ directory exists but appears to be legacy/reference material)
integration_requirements: No module registration required (pure CSS change)
